name: Hetch Hetchy / O'Shaughnessy Dam
summary_en: The archive documents trace the O'Shaughnessy Dam and Hetch Hetchy system. A 2005 history records that San Francisco approved a "$45 million bond to build Hetch Hetchy System" in 1910, that the dam was built 1919-1923, and that it was later "raised to store 360,360 acre feet." A 1975-76 annual report of the Hetch Hetchy Water and Power System notes the dam was raised "an additional 85.5 feet," the system cost "some $100 million," and water reached Crystal Springs Reservoir in 1934. The system is operated by the Public Utilities Commission, whose FY2025 budget line is about $1.8B; a city contract for "Auto Parts for Hetch Hetchy" with Pine Mountain Auto Inc has been paid $28,748.78.

documents.title: San Francisco water and power : a history of the Municipal Water Department & Hetch Hetchy system
documents.creator: Hanson, Warren D,Kukula, Frank,Nelson, Christopher,Williams, Mary

documents.gloss: A 2005 SFPL history of the Municipal Water Department and Hetch Hetchy System, whose timeline dates the O'Shaughnessy Dam to 1919-1923 and its raising to store 360,360 acre feet.

documents.title: San Francisco water and power : a history of the Municipal Water Department and Hetch Hetchy system

documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1999 edition of the same municipal water history, tracing the Hetch Hetchy System from the 1913 Raker Act to the delivery of Sierra Nevada water by 1934.
